subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request from user u_9981 (senior-engineer role) to production database db-payments-primary against emergency access policy, role-based access controls, and incident escalation governance.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask (mid tier) is appropriate because this is an explicit governance decision: the query asks for policy evaluation, access control approval, and compliance checking against a policy registry. A mid tier is justified because the evaluation requires multi-signal reasoning (user role, target resource classification, incident severity context, and emergency access policy interaction) rather than a simple single-rule check. No additional subtasks are needed; this is a pure access-control decision that does not require data reconciliation, SQL execution, or multi-tool orchestration."

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) has an active P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042, satisfying POL-501's incident ticket requirement. POL-502 dual approval is waived under the declared P1 incident exception. POL-503 requires post-access audit logging and review within 24 hours.
applied_constraints: Access is time-limited to the duration of incident INC-20241215-0042 only.
All read/write operations on db-payments-primary must be fully logged with session recording enabled.
Access grant and all actions must be reviewed by DBA team and security within 24 hours of incident closure.
User u_9981 must provide incident activity summary upon access termination.
Access must be revoked immediately upon P1 incident resolution or escalation closure.
